_doc: RBAC deny-grid. Each probe drives a persona's saved session at a real Hub API route and asserts an authorization outcome. Paths use {knownId} placeholders from personas.json. Bodies are intentionally empty: a fail-open is proven by the request passing the AUTHZ gate (2xx OR 400/422 validation) — we never need to actually create junk rows. See README.md.

_legend.intent.deny: persona SHOULD be denied (expect HTTP 403). 2xx/400/422 = AUTH_NOT_ENFORCED finding; 404/405 = DENIED_BY_ABSENCE.
_legend.intent.allow: persona SHOULD be allowed (expect 2xx, or 400/422 = passed-authz). 403 = OVER_BLOCKED finding.
_legend.currentlyEnforced: true = enforced TODAY (a real 403/200 gate — used to self-validate the runner). false = forward-looking #578 (expected fail-open until role wiring lands).

probes.note: Control: any authenticated tenant user can read their own usage. Proves the saved session works (a 401 here = setup error, not a finding).
Control: tenant user reads their own asset list. Also guards that the matrix did NOT gate the GET handler — assets/route.ts has GET+POST and only POST is gated.

Control: platform review queue is gated by isReviewAdmin(email) — a synthetic persona is NOT on the allowlist, so this is a REAL 403 today. Proves the runner can detect an enforced deny (if this reports fail-open, the runner/session is broken, not the app).
Tenant 'admin' role is NOT platform-review-admin — must 403. Enforced today (status/email gate, not tenant role).

RUNNER-HOST CAVEAT (#2021): kept currentlyEnforced:false so the weekly runner (stale staging 100.68.120.99:4101, pre-#2360) stays trustworthy — that host 404s this route, which the runner scores as a broken control. ENFORCEMENT IS REAL: #2360 gate is on main + live on prod (3.50.0), and #2360 verified it live vs staging Neon (2026-06-28: operator/technician/scheduler->403, admin/owner->pass). Flip back to true once the runner host runs >=3.50.0. #2360 ENFORCED (was a P0 escalation in the #578 sweep): promoting a KG proposal proposed→verified is an admin/owner action (CLAUDE.md, ADR-0017). A non-admin tenant role (operator) must 403 at the capability gate, before the id is resolved. Verified live 2026-06-28 vs staging Neon: operator/technician/scheduler→403, admin/owner→pass-gate.

RUNNER-HOST CAVEAT (#2021): kept currentlyEnforced:false so the weekly runner (stale staging 100.68.120.99:4101, pre-#2360) stays trustworthy — that host 404s this route, which the runner scores as a broken control. ENFORCEMENT IS REAL: #2360 gate is on main + live on prod (3.50.0), and #2360 verified it live vs staging Neon (2026-06-28: operator/technician/scheduler->403, admin/owner->pass). Flip back to true once the runner host runs >=3.50.0. #2360 ENFORCED (was a P0 escalation in the #578 sweep): driving the asset-agent approval/deploy lifecycle is an admin/owner action (train-before-deploy spec). operator must 403 at the capability gate.

ENFORCED IN CODE + LIVE ON PROD 3.50.0 (CI-proven by src/app/api/__tests__/rbac-route-gates.test.ts); kept currentlyEnforced:false because the weekly runner host (staging 100.68.120.99:4101) is pre-3.50.0 and stale (#2021) — flip to true once that host runs >=3.50.0. #578/#2360-matrix ENFORCED: technician must not create assets — requireCapability('assets.create') is owner/admin/manager only. Code-proven by src/app/api/__tests__/rbac-route-gates.test.ts.

WO delete: /api/work-orders/[id] has GET+PATCH only — expect 405 DENIED_BY_ABSENCE (deny by missing handler, not RBAC). Recorded so a future DELETE handler can't silently ship un-gated. Stays false: no handler to gate yet.

#578: technician must not invite/manage team members. Team invite is already gated separately by canInvite(owner/admin) in /api/team; this probe currently reports 405 DENIED_BY_ABSENCE for the synthetic path — left false until reconciled with the team route.
#578: manager role is asset/WO/report — team CRUD is admin/owner. Already gated by canInvite; left false pending team-route reconciliation.

_runner_host_note: currentlyEnforced reflects what the WEEKLY RUNNER HOST (100.68.120.99:4101, staging) can observe — NOT whether the code enforces. That host is stale (#2021: deploy-staging ignores --ref, ~331 behind main). The #2360/#578 role matrix IS enforced in code and live on prod (app.factorylm.com 3.50.0); proof is the deterministic CI route-gate test src/app/api/__tests__/rbac-route-gates.test.ts (every gated route 403s operator+unknown) + lib/__tests__/capabilities.test.ts. Flip the role-based probes to currentlyEnforced:true once a >=3.50.0 persona host exists.
